description_long: This warehouse is a large, rectangular building with a prominent central entrance flanked by two symmetrical towers, each topped with a pitched roof. The main facade features arched windows and decorative stonework, with a circular window above the main doorway. The structure sits on a wide concrete base, and the roof is steeply gabled, extending the full length of the building. The side views reveal the building's considerable length and the utilitarian, windowless expanse of its main body, while the towers provide architectural interest and likely serve as observation or access points. The exterior appears to be constructed primarily of concrete or stone, with weathered textures and muted gray tones dominating the palette.
description: A large, rectangular warehouse with twin towers, arched windows, and a steep gabled roof, finished in gray stone and concrete textures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, arched windows, circular window, and both towers with pitched roofs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the building's long, windowless expanse and the side profile of the towers.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the gabled roof and the symmetrical placement of the towers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the length of the building and the towers' side profiles.
attribute_facade_design: The main facade features a central arched entryway, flanked by two symmetrical towers with pitched roofs and arched windows. Decorative stonework and a circular window above the entrance add architectural detail, while the facade is finished in textured gray stone and concrete.
attribute_roof_structure: The roof is steeply gabled, constructed from what appears to be metal or slate panels, and extends the full length of the building. The towers are topped with smaller, pitched roofs made of similar materials.
